title: Underfloor Heating Calculator
shortDescription: Estimate pipe length and loop count for water-based underfloor heating.
metaTitle: Underfloor Heating Calculator | Pipe Length & Loop Count
metaDescription: Estimate how much pipe you need for underfloor heating, how many loops are required, and the average loop length based on area, spacing, and waste.
intro: Use this underfloor heating calculator to estimate pipe length, number of loops, and average loop length for a water-based system. It is a practical starting point for planning material takeoff and answering the question: how much pipe for underfloor heating?
overview: Enter the heated area, pipe spacing, maximum loop length, extra connection length, and waste allowance to get a fast estimate of total pipe length and loop quantity.
howItWorks: Enter the heated floor area in square meters.
Set the pipe spacing in centimeters.
Specify the maximum allowed loop length.
Add the extra connection length for manifold runs.
Include a waste percentage for cutting and layout margins.
useCases: Estimating how much pipe is needed for underfloor heating.
Planning the number of heating loops before installation.
Checking whether a floor area can be covered within loop length limits.
Preparing a quick material estimate for a renovation or new build.

examples.title: Calculation example
examples.input: 100 m2 area, 15 cm spacing, 100 m max loop length, 5 m extra length, 10% waste
examples.output: Approximate pipe length, loop count, and average loop length are calculated from the entered values.
examples.description: Shows a typical planning scenario for a water underfloor heating system.

examples.title: Material planning
examples.input: Use the result to compare with manifold capacity and pipe roll lengths
examples.output: Helps avoid underestimating the amount of pipe and number of circuits needed.

faq.question: How accurate is this underfloor heating calculator?
faq.answer: It provides a practical estimate for planning. Final values can vary depending on room shape, edge zones, manifold layout, and installation method.

faq.question: What does the pipe spacing affect?
faq.answer: Smaller spacing increases the total pipe length, while larger spacing reduces it. The calculator uses spacing to estimate pipe demand from the heated area.

faq.question: Why do I need extra connection length?
faq.answer: Extra connection length covers the runs from the manifold to the heated area and back. This helps make the estimate more realistic.

faq.question: What if the loop length exceeds the limit?
faq.answer: The calculator will indicate more loops are needed so each circuit stays within the maximum loop length you set.

faq.question: Can I use this for electric floor heating?
faq.answer: This calculator is designed for water-based underfloor heating pipe planning, not electric heating mats or cables.

seo.title: Underfloor Heating Calculator
seo.description: Estimate pipe length, number of loops, and average loop length for water underfloor heating based on area, spacing, and waste.
keywords: how much pipe for underfloor heating
